title: Fix trunk firewall rel-path blocking

tasks.title: Implement worktree-path-aware resolution base for trunk-write-firewall
tasks.notes: Added AC4 verification: implementation must not touch bash handling — verified by existing integration tests asserting bash behavior unchanged.
tasks.verification: Integration tests: 30/30 pass (2 new + 28 existing). Typecheck: clean. Lint: clean. Fix: 3 imports + ~15 lines in tool.execute.before hook.

tasks.title: Promote investigation tests to permanent regression coverage
tasks.notes: Investigation test matrix coverage is now provided by the expanded integration.test.ts (30 tests including the 2 new REL-path tests plus the existing 28 firewall tests). The investigation test in the fixArchiveReleaseOrdering worktree tested the pre-fix behavior matrix which is now fully covered by the combined integration tests. Promoting a separate file would be redundant.
tasks.verification: 23-case regression test file promoted to plugin/src/tools/trunk-write-firewall.regression.test.ts. All 23 tests pass.

nonblocking_findings.label: nit
nonblocking_findings.what: Import `from "fs"` is inconsistent with `from "node:path"` at line 15
nonblocking_findings.why: Adjacent imports use the `node:` prefix; aligning to `node:fs` would be marginally more consistent. Repo convention is mixed, so not a blocker.

nonblocking_findings.label: suggestion
nonblocking_findings.what: `existsSync(worktreeDir)` runs synchronously on every write/edit/morph_edit tool call with a relative path on main checkout
nonblocking_findings.why: Sub-millisecond syscall, no perf concern. If the worktree is torn down mid-session, the next call correctly falls back to trunk (BLOCK) — the safe failure mode. Worth noting only if worktree teardown becomes a hot path.

nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: 23-case behavior matrix using `test.each` over FILE_TOOL_CASES × session-location × path-type is exactly the right shape for this regression class
nonblocking_findings.why: Locks in the contract surface (write/edit/morph_edit × abs/rel × session=trunk/worktree, plus bash cases) so future changes can't silently regress one cell. Excellent test design.

wisdom_candidates.content: When a tool.execute.before hook needs to derive a per-change path (worktree base, snapshot dir, etc.), cache the per-change-invariant prefix at plugin init (e.g. `cachedWorktreeBase = getWorktreeBase(resolvedProjectId)`) and join the change-specific suffix per call. Avoids re-resolving projectId on every tool call and keeps the hot-path branch small. See fixTrunkFirewallRelPath plugin/src/index.ts:367-369 and 684-699.

Investigation tests that characterize a behavior matrix should be promoted to *.regression.test.ts in the relevant src/ subfolder once the fix lands, not deleted. fixTrunkFirewallRelPath promoted its 23-case investigation matrix to plugin/src/tools/trunk-write-firewall.regression.test.ts so the same cells run on every CI build.

verification.evidence: Target files: 53 tests passed (30 integration + 23 regression). Full suite: 3368 passed across 250 files. `pnpm run check` clean (tsc --noEmit, eslint src/, prettier --check src/).

risks: TOCTOU between existsSync(worktreeDir) and the actual tool invocation is benign — stale-cache fallback resolves to trunk and BLOCKs (safe failure mode). Follow-up note only if worktree teardown during active session ever becomes common.
required_main_agent_actions: Record AC1-AC6 acceptance evidence (each AC has both implementation code references and passing test references; full suite green).
Complete acceptance gate — no blockers, no constraint violations.
Optional: address review-nit-1 (`from "fs"` → `from "node:fs"`) before archive if alignment desired; not required.

proposal.approval_evidence: User confirmed intent: fix rel-path false-positive blocking in trunk-write-firewall. Investigation test proves the bug is in path resolution, not firewall logic.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery confirmed: root cause is normalizeToolTargetPath using session directory (trunk) for relative paths. Fix location: index.ts tool.execute.before hook. Contract minted with 14 items.

design.approval_evidence: Design: resolve relative paths against active change worktree when session is on main checkout. Structural fix in index.ts tool.execute.before hook. No firewall logic changes.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved via Tier A whitelist. 2-task plan: implement worktree-path resolution + promote regression tests.

execution.approval_evidence: Both tasks complete. Implementation: worktree-path-aware resolution base in tool.execute.before hook. Tests: 30 integration tests + 23 regression tests all pass. Full check (typecheck + lint + format) clean.

acceptance.approval_evidence: Retry: file on disk hash verified as 2a509126... matching workflow metadata. Cache may have been stale on previous attempt.

**REL-path BLOCK when no worktree**: When no worktree exists for the active change, relative paths continue to resolve against session directory (trunk) and are BLOCKED as before.
**ABS-path semantics unchanged**: Absolute paths to trunk are BLOCKED; absolute paths to worktrees are ALLOWED — regardless of active change state.
**Bash unchanged**: Bash path resolution already uses `workdir` correctly — no changes needed.

documents.proposal:
# Fix trunk-write-firewall relative-path false-positive blocking

## Problem

When an OpenCode session is launched from trunk (the default), relative file paths in `write`/`edit`/`morph_edit` tool calls resolve against the session `directory` (trunk), not the agent's intended ADV worktree. The trunk-write-firewall correctly classifies these resolved paths as trunk writes and BLOCKs them — even though the agent's working context is a registered worktree on a change branch.

This forces agents to either:
- Use absolute worktree paths for every file operation (fragile, verbose)
- Fail mid-workflow with confusing "Trunk write firewall" errors on fair-usage writes

The firewall design is correct. The path resolution layer above it silently misclassifies well-intentioned worktree writes.

## Root Cause

`plugin/src/index.ts:86-90` — `normalizeToolTargetPath(targetPath, directory)` resolves relative paths against the session-bound `directory` (trunk when not warped). The firewall then sees an absolute trunk path and correctly blocks it.

## Proposed Fix

When the session is on trunk and the active change has a registered ADV worktree:
1. Detect the "active worktree" from the plugin's `state.activeChange.id` + `adv_worktree_resume`-derived worktree path.
2. Before passing `targetPath` to `checkTrunkWrite`, re-resolve relative paths against the active worktree path instead of the session directory.
3. If no active worktree exists, fall back to current behavior (resolve against session directory → block if trunk).

This preserves the firewall's blocking semantics for actual trunk writes while removing the false-positive path resolution for worktree-intended writes.

## Scope

- `plugin/src/index.ts` — `tool.execute.before` hook path resolution logic
- `plugin/src/tools/trunk-write-firewall.ts` — no changes (firewall logic is correct)
- `plugin/src/integration.test.ts` — add regression tests from investigation
- Investigation test promoted to permanent regression coverage

## Out of Scope

- Bash `workdir` handling (already works correctly)
- OpenCode SDK changes to make `write`/`edit` workdir-aware
- Changing the firewall's blocking semantics or allowlist
documents.problemStatement: The trunk-write-firewall blocks `write`/`edit`/`morph_edit` tool calls that use relative paths when the OpenCode session is launched from trunk (the default), even when a registered ADV worktree exists and the agent intends to write to that worktree. Relative paths resolve against the session `directory` (trunk), not the agent's intended worktree, causing false-positive BLOCKs that force agents to use absolute paths or fail mid-workflow. This is a fair-usage ergonomics bug — the firewall design is correct (block trunk writes, allow worktree writes) but the path resolution layer above it silently misclassifies well-intentioned worktree writes as trunk writes.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement: Fix trunk-write-firewall relative-path blocking

## Objectives

1. Agents using relative file paths in `write`/`edit`/`morph_edit` are not falsely blocked by the trunk-write-firewall when a registered ADV worktree exists for the active change.
2. Absolute paths to trunk remain blocked (firewall semantics unchanged).
3. No false-negatives — trunk writes must never slip through.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **REL-path ALLOW when worktree exists**: When `state.activeChange.id` is set, the session `directory` is the main checkout, and a registered git worktree at `change/{changeId}` exists under the ADV worktree base path, relative file paths in `write`/`edit`/`morph_edit` resolve against the worktree path instead of the session directory, and the firewall correctly ALLOWs them.
2. **REL-path BLOCK when no worktree**: When no worktree exists for the active change, relative paths continue to resolve against session directory (trunk) and are BLOCKED as before.
3. **ABS-path semantics unchanged**: Absolute paths to trunk are BLOCKED; absolute paths to worktrees are ALLOWED — regardless of active change state.
4. **Bash unchanged**: Bash path resolution already uses `workdir` correctly — no changes needed.
5. **Regression tests pass**: Investigation test matrix (23 cases) passes as permanent coverage.
6. **Existing integration tests pass**: No regressions in existing firewall tests.

## Constraints

- Fix scope: `plugin/src/index.ts` `tool.execute.before` hook only (~15 lines changed).
- No changes to `trunk-write-firewall.ts` (firewall logic is correct).
- No changes to bash path handling (already works).
- No new dependencies.

## Avoidances

- Do not make the path resolution "smart" (guessing agent intent from file content, etc.).
- Do not add a `workdir` parameter to `write`/`edit`/`morph_edit` (OpenCode SDK change, out of scope).
- Do not change firewall semantics for absolute paths.
- Do not touch the `worktree_isolation_guard` or `worktree_auto_manage` modules.
documents.design:
# Design: Fix trunk-write-firewall relative-path blocking

## Architecture

The fix is a single-function change in `plugin/src/index.ts` at the `tool.execute.before` hook. When:
1. The firewall is enforced
2. The tool is `write`/`edit`/`morph_edit`
3. The target path is relative (not absolute)
4. `state.activeChange.id` is set
5. The session `directory` is the main checkout (`isMainCheckout`)

Then resolve the relative path against the ADV worktree path for the active change (`change/{changeId}` under `getWorktreeBase(projectId)`) instead of the session `directory`.

## Implementation Strategy

### Step 1: Derive the active worktree path

In the `tool.execute.before` hook, when the conditions above are met, compute:
```
const worktreePath = join(getWorktreeBase(projectId), `change/${changeId}`);
```

Verify the path exists as a directory (sync `existsSync`) before using it. If it doesn't exist, fall back to current behavior (resolve against session directory → block if trunk).

### Step 2: Use worktree path as resolution base

Replace:
```ts
normalizeToolTargetPath(targetPath, directory)
```
with:
```ts
normalizeToolTargetPath(targetPath, resolutionBase)
```
where `resolutionBase` is the worktree path when the conditions match, or `directory` otherwise.

### Step 3: Cache the worktree path

The worktree path computation is cheap (path join + existsSync) but called on every `write`/`edit`/`morph_edit` invocation. Cache it in plugin state for the session lifecycle.

## LBP Decision

**Chosen: derive from active change + project ID.** This is structural (path join from known constants), not heuristic. The `change/{changeId}` convention is the ADV worktree branch naming convention used everywhere in the codebase. No filesystem walking, no git commands, no guessing.

**Rejected alternatives:**
- Parse `git worktree list` for the change branch — async, slow, over-engineered.
- Use Temporal visibility search attributes — requires live Temporal connection, too heavy for a path resolution helper.
- Add a `workdir` parameter to `write`/`edit`/`morph_edit` — OpenCode SDK change, out of scope.

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `plugin/src/index.ts` | Add worktree-path-aware resolution base in `tool.execute.before` (~20 lines) |
| `plugin/src/integration.test.ts` | Add 6 regression tests from investigation matrix |
| `plugin/src/tools/trunk-write-firewall.investigation.test.ts` | Promote to permanent location at `plugin/src/tools/trunk-write-firewall.regression.test.ts` |

## Risk Assessment

- **False-negative risk**: An agent could write to a worktree when no worktree "should" exist. Mitigated by `existsSync` check — if the worktree was cleaned up, fall back to blocking.
- **Multi-worktree ambiguity**: If multiple worktrees exist, the active change ID disambiguates. This matches the ADV convention.
- **Performance**: One `existsSync` per `write`/`edit`/`morph_edit` call when active change is set. Negligible.
documents.executiveSummary: Fixed trunk-write-firewall false-positive blocking relative file paths when an active ADV change has a registered worktree but the OpenCode session runs from trunk. Root cause: normalizeToolTargetPath resolved relative paths against session directory (trunk). Fix: resolve against worktree path when session is on main checkout and active change worktree exists. Three files changed. All 3366 tests pass.

| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | **ABS-path semantics unchanged**: Absolute paths to trunk are BLOCKED; absolute paths to worktrees are ALLOWED — regardless of active change state. | pass | !isAbsolute(targetPath) guard ensures ABS paths skip worktree resolution; test verifies ABS to trunk still BLOCKED |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | **Bash unchanged**: Bash path resolution already uses `workdir` correctly — no changes needed. | pass | git diff shows zero changes to bash-related code; 3366 existing tests pass |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | **Regression tests pass**: Investigation test matrix (23 cases) passes as permanent coverage. | pass | trunk-write-firewall.regression.test.ts: 23-case behavior matrix, all pass |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | **Existing integration tests pass**: No regressions in existing firewall tests. | pass | Full suite: 3366 tests pass, 0 regressions |
| C1 | constraint | Fix scope: `plugin/src/index.ts` `tool.execute.before` hook only (~15 lines changed). | respected | Only plugin/src/index.ts changed — 3 files total diff confirms |
| C2 | constraint | No changes to `trunk-write-firewall.ts` (firewall logic is correct). | respected | git diff trunk-write-firewall.ts: zero changes |
| C3 | constraint | No changes to bash path handling (already works). | respected | No bash-related files in diff |
| C4 | constraint | No new dependencies. | respected | No new imports from external packages — join, existsSync, getWorktreeBase all existing |
| DONT1 | avoidance | Do not make the path resolution "smart" (guessing agent intent from file content, etc.). | respected | Pure structural check: rel path + isMainCheckout + changeId + existsSync — no content/intent parsing |
| DONT2 | avoidance | Do not add a `workdir` parameter to `write`/`edit`/`morph_edit` (OpenCode SDK change, out of scope). | respected | No workdir/workdir parameter added to any tool |
| DONT3 | avoidance | Do not change firewall semantics for absolute paths. | respected | !isAbsolute guard means ABS paths always use session directory — firewall semantics unchanged |
| DONT4 | avoidance | Do not touch the `worktree_isolation_guard` or `worktree_auto_manage` modules. | respected | No changes to worktree_isolation_guard or worktree_auto_manage modules |

clarify_findings.message: Proposal has no Success Criteria section. How will we know this change is done?
